Now we've got so many different targeting options available to us, including inside of AdWords, we're going to start building your ad campaigns for your YouTube ads. There's all these different parameters you can put in place. You can do it based on parental status as well. Do they have kids or not? You can build similar audiences, which is rather like lookalike audiences on Facebook. You can do affinity audiences, which are rather like interest groups on Facebook. You can do channel placements. So if you wanted to run your ads in front of people when they're searching certain types of videos or watching certain types of videos, which will be bracketed into different types of topics, and of course, you can run your ads there as well. ## [03:00 - 06:03]

ones for people looking up different CRMs. So if you're looking up lots of different CRM options, then you'll fall into an in-market audience of people looking up CRMs. And so it makes your life very easy as an advertiser if you want to get in front of that type of audience. Likewise, you can do website remarketing, which is exactly the same as website retargeting on Facebook. So many different options available here, so many others as well that haven't been mentioned here. So there's so many different targeting options available to you. But I'm going to show you which ones you should probably focus on first. Likewise, there are two different types of video ads that you probably want to pay attention to. You've got the in-stream ads, which are the ads that appear before you're about to watch other videos. So you've probably seen these before. They're often called pre-roll ads. You press play on a video, and as soon as that video plays, a pre-roll ad pops up before, and you have to wait for that five seconds before you can skip the app button. Typically, most people get quite frustrated by these ads, but as long as you create an ad that's actually useful for your audience at a time where they would like to see your ad and you provide them with good quality content, you'll see the comments that you get are typically very positive. I love seeing this ad. This is amazing. I love the content you shared with it. Not I hate you, which is typically most YouTube comments on the internet. So you've got in-stream, and then you've also got discovery. Discovery ads are the ones that appear at the top of the search results or on the right hand side. But you can get your videos appearing on the right hand side of other videos as well, and they can work particularly well as well. So different types of ads. In-stream ads, funnily enough, you only pay when someone watches past 30 seconds or decides to click to your website. So you're only really paying for a very engaged viewer. Whereas with discovery ads, you pay as soon as someone decides to click the thumbnail of your video to play it. So you pay for engagement with YouTube advertising. if they're ready to make a purchase, then in-market audiences are good.
